title: OpenClaw Platform Onboarding Template v1.0.0
description: Checklist + config skeleton for adding any new platform to the OpenClaw multi-browser runtime. Copy + fill to onboard a new platform in <1 day.
_onboarding_instructions: 2. Fill all REQUIRED fields (marked with // REQUIRED)
3. Complete the connector_checklist items in order
4. Run smoke tests, then integration tests, then safety tests
5. Add the platform lane to config/platform-lanes.json
6. Add or update a browser profile in config/browser-profiles.json
8. Add skill to owning agent in config/openclaw.json
9. Update AGENTS.md to reflect new capabilities

platform.category: social | crm | content | community | ecommerce | analytics | other

credentials.notes: Never hardcode credentials. Use env vars only.

rollback.strategy: describe rollback approach (e.g. archive vs delete, unpublish vs delete)

skill_file.selectors_source: Inspect platform DOM manually in sandbox profile. Document in SELECTORS object.

skills_registry_entry.replay_policy: manual replay only with operation ledger

phase_1_discovery.task: Confirm if API exists and what it covers vs browser-only gaps
Determine session TTL and cookie scope
Identify risk tier for each operation

phase_2_config.task: Assign or create browser profile in config/browser-profiles.json
Verify approval-policies.json covers all risk tiers used

phase_3_skill_implementation.task: Inspect platform DOM in sandbox, fill SELECTORS object
Implement login command with session save/load
Implement all read commands (no approval required)
Implement all write commands behind governance gate
Implement critical/destructive commands with approvedBy param
Add Telegram notify() on all destructive/publish operations

phase_4_registry_and_agents.task: Add skill to owning agent's skills[] in config/openclaw.json

phase_5_testing.task: Smoke test: run login command against sandbox-test profile
Smoke test: run screenshot command and confirm Telegram notification
Integration test: trigger a gated write — confirm governance blocks it without approval
Integration test: grant approval via Telegram, confirm write executes
Safety test: trigger same action 6x to verify anti-loop kicks in at max_same_action_per_window
Safety test: set OPENCLAW_AUTONOMOUS_PAUSED=1, confirm all write ops block
Verify audit trail: check logs/platform-ops-audit.jsonl for all test ops

phase_6_go_live.task: Set live env vars (PLATFORM_SLUG_EMAIL, PLATFORM_SLUG_PASSWORD, etc.)
Run login command against live profile — confirm session persists
Run one low-risk read op against live profile
Confirm Telegram channel is active for the platform's approval routing
Document rollback plan: how to undo first N ops if something goes wrong
Update SOUL.md or MEMORY.md with platform summary and key selectors

integration_tests.name: Approved write executes and is recorded in audit log
Idempotency dedup on repeated identical op

safety_tests.name: Critical op with wrong confirmation phrase is rejected

security_checklist.check: No credentials hardcoded — all from env vars
Session files stored in data/browser-sessions/ (not commited to git)
buildBrowserLaunchArgs() called on every browser launch
No eval(), no dynamic import of untrusted input
All user-provided strings sanitized before use in page.fill()
Audit log entries contain no raw credentials or session tokens
Telegram channel is private and token is not publicly exposed

rollback_plan.first_24h: Keep sandbox-test profile active; run all writes in dry_run mode first
rollback_plan.if_audit_log_overflows: Rotate logs/platform-ops-audit.jsonl to a dated archive file
rollback_plan.if_anti_loop_fires_in_prod: Set OPENCLAW_AUTONOMOUS_PAUSED=1, investigate root cause, clear data/runtime/platform-anti-loop-ledger.json, unpause
rollback_plan.if_bad_publish: Manually revert via platform UI; record corrective action in audit log under correlation_id
